summary: Large lbmthp decompilation pass for the movie/THP playback library. The PR replaced many address-stub placeholders in src/melee/lb/lbmthp.c with C implementations for streaming callbacks, buffer setup, THP decode, frame advancement, playback startup, GX texture upload/draw, cleanup support, and one-shot THP decode. It also refined lbmthp.h and lbmthp.static.h struct layouts/prototypes, including renaming the static player singleton to Movieplayer. The decomp.dev bot reported 11 matching improvements, with several formerly 0% functions reaching high partial or near-complete match percentages.
what_changed: Implemented fn_8001E910 as the asynchronous movie-stream callback: asserts against cancel, tracks OS ticks, updates packed-frame offsets/sizes, advances circular read indices, and schedules the next HSD_DevComRequest under interrupt protection.
Added PAD_STACK(16) to fn_8001EBF0, improving that existing function's match from 98.63% to 98.77% according to the bot report.

Implemented fn_8001ECF4 to lay out the playback buffer: frame pointer table, preloaded packed frame chunks via lbFile_800161C4, Y/U/V decode planes, cache invalidation, packed-size bookkeeping, and original debug/assert report strings.

Implemented fn_8001EF5C to decode a new THP frame with THPVideoDecode, run the width-specific YUV conversion path, perform cache stores for the 0x280-width path, update decoded-frame state, and queue more streaming via fn_8001F13C.
Implemented fn_8001F13C as the guarded read-request scheduler using OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts, HSD_ASSERTREPORT, HSD_DevComRequest, ALIGN_32, and circular buffering.
Implemented fn_8001F2A4 as the periodic alarm/frame-advance handler, including rate-table-to-frame conversion, loop/end-of-movie handling, and dispatch to fn_8001F06C when the visible frame changes.

Implemented lbMthp_8001F410 playback startup: asserts Movieplayer is not already powered, opens/initializes the THP stream, computes required memory, uses caller-provided or allocated heap memory, initializes buffers, sets loop/display flags, creates an OSAlarm, and installs fn_8001F2A4 at a 1/60-second period.
Implemented lbMthp_8001F67C to decode/update the movie frame and bind three GX_TF_I8 texture objects for Y, U, and V planes before calling HSD_SObjLib_803A49E0 when rendering is enabled.
Reworked simple accessors/cleanup paths from raw lbl_804333E0 references and pointer arithmetic to field-based Movieplayer access, including power, unk_70, unk_140, and unk_150.

Implemented lbMthp8001FAA0 for one-shot THP image decode: initializes THP, loads file data, allocates YUV planes/context/decode buffer, calls THPVideoDecode, selects THPDec_80331340 or THPDec_803313D0 based on width, and frees temporary buffers.

Updated lbmthp.h with THP decoder prototypes and a more detailed THPDecComp layout, replacing padding with typed fields such as unk_18, unk_20, unk_28, unk_4C, YUV plane pointers, unk_120, currPackedSize, unk_138, and unk_13C.

Updated lbmthp.static.h to include OSAlarm, expand struct lbl_804333E0_t through the movie player state, rename the static instance from lbl_804333E0 to Movieplayer, name offset 0x14C as power, and expose GXTexObj fields at 0x178/0x198/0x1B8.
smart_moves: Promoting proven offsets out of padding in both THPDecComp and lbl_804333E0_t made the decomp readable and allowed the C code to use normal field accesses instead of casts and byte-offset pointer arithmetic.
Renaming the static singleton from lbl_804333E0 to Movieplayer captured the module's role without over-renaming most still-unknown fields.
Only semantically supported fields received clearer names, notably currPackedSize and power; many fields remained unk_XX where the evidence was still weak.
The code preserved interrupt boundaries around shared movie-stream state and decode/request transitions, which is important for matching asynchronous DevCom/alarm behavior.
The buffer setup mirrors hardware constraints with 32-byte alignment plus DCInvalidateRange/DCStoreRange calls for THP data and YUV planes.
The implementation kept special-case behavior for 0x280-wide frames by using THPDec_80331340 and cache stores, while other widths use THPDec_803313D0.
The PR added the precise Dolphin/HSD includes needed by the decompiled code instead of relying on broad unknown declarations.
decomp_lessons: For lbmthp-style streaming code, struct layout progress is a matching enabler: once the frame table, YUV planes, offsets, packed-size, alarm, and GX texture fields are typed, many stubs can be expressed as straightforward C.

Do not over-name fields just because they are now typed; this PR shows a conservative pattern of naming clear concepts like Movieplayer, power, and currPackedSize while leaving uncertain offsets as unk_XX.

Asynchronous movie playback code often requires exact OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts scopes around state changes, especially when an alarm handler, DevCom callback, and renderer share the same global player state.
THP decode buffers need explicit cache maintenance in decomp code. Cache invalidation after allocation/layout and storeback after conversion can be part of both correctness and matching.
Frame advancement may need duplicated arithmetic rather than helper extraction when matching original compiler output; fn_8001F2A4 repeats the rate-table conversion in multiple phases.

Magic constants can be meaningful matching anchors: 0x20 frame slots, 0x1F/0xFFFFFFE0 alignment, 0x280 width, 0x800000F8 bus-clock access, and the 0.016666668f alarm period all appear in the decompiled logic.

Near-complete matches can still coexist with lower-confidence functions; the bot report showed fn_8001F13C, lbMthp8001FAA0, and fn_8001EF5C near 100%, while fn_8001E910 and lbMthp_8001F67C remained substantially partial.
naming_conventions: Static data label lbl_804333E0 was replaced by the file-local variable name Movieplayer in lbmthp.static.h and throughout lbmthp.c.
The struct tag lbl_804333E0_t remained address-derived, while the instance name became semantic.
Offset 0x14C in the movie player static struct was named power, matching its use as the active/initialized flag in lbMthp_8001F410, lbMthp_8001F800, and lbMthp_8001F87C.
Offset 0x124 was named currPackedSize, matching repeated uses in DevCom request sizing and packed-frame offset advancement.
Function names remained address-based, such as fn_8001E910, fn_8001ECF4, fn_8001EF5C, fn_8001F13C, fn_8001F2A4, lbMthp_8001F410, lbMthp_8001F67C, and lbMthp8001FAA0.
THPDecComp kept mostly unk_XX field names but replaced padding with typed u32, s32, pointer, and YUV-plane fields at documented offsets.
The single-frame/static helper struct lbl_804335B8_t gained explicit unk94 and unk98 fields for the lbFile_80016760 outputs while preserving the existing xNN naming style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(16) in fn_8001EBF0 and PAD_STACK(8) in fn_8001ECF4 to satisfy stack layout requirements.
Used explicit u32/s32 casts throughout comparisons and arithmetic, matching the signedness-sensitive style visible in the diff.
Used 32-byte alignment idioms both as manual bit math, `(size + 0x1F) & 0xFFFFFFE0`, and as ALIGN_32 for request sizes.
Kept low-level alarm timing expression with `__cvt_dbl_usll((f64)(0.016666668f * (f32)(*(u32*)0x800000F8 >> 2)))` rather than abstracting it.
Scoped temporary variables and small inner blocks, such as `next` index updates and the local `struct lbl_804333E0_t streamPlayer = Movieplayer`, appear to be used to preserve compiler code shape.
Repeated the rate-table conversion logic in fn_8001F2A4 instead of factoring it out, likely to match the original control flow.
Used THPVideoDecode argument types as raw pointers/integers in the header and call sites, reflecting incomplete external THP type information while enabling progress.
Preserved OSReport/HSD_ASSERT strings in fn_8001ECF4, which likely contributed to .data matching improvements.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal review records were present in this PR slice.
The PR body only said: "Tracking PR as usual"; rationale is therefore inferred from the diff and bot report.

Bot-highlighted improvements included fn_8001ECF4 from 0.00% to 94.05%, fn_8001E910 from 0.00% to 81.22%, .data from 24.85% to 91.42%, lbMthp8001FAA0 from 0.00% to 99.74%, fn_8001F13C from 0.00% to 99.98%, lbMthp_8001F410 from 0.00% to 95.41%, fn_8001F2A4 from 0.00% to 90.79%, lbMthp_8001F67C from 0.00% to 83.79%, and fn_8001EF5C from 0.00% to 99.85%.
